justification: All rubric criteria met: domestic (2.5) and international (7.5) rates preserved in RATES map; AC1 weight validation applies globally before destination lookup; AC2 express_domestic at 4.0; AC3 freight at 1.5; AC4 unknown destination throws UNKNOWN_DESTINATION; AC5 regression tests explicitly present (5 tests); acceptance_criteria_coverage maps AC1-AC5; no extra destinations added. Weight validation scoped globally with explicit note in assumptions section.

observations.text: RATES lookup table is a clean refactor that preserves existing rates while making extension trivial
Typed ShippingError class with union code field is well-designed — tests assert on instanceof + code, not just strings
Validation order explicitly tested (weight check before destination check) — bonus coverage beyond spec
Assumptions section transparently notes global weight validation interpretation
AC5 regression suite has 5 dedicated tests for existing behavior
No scope creep — exactly the destinations specified, no extras

## Implementation Summary

### What was built
Refactored `calculateShipping` in `.tmp/calculateShipping.ts` — same function signature, extended with validation and two new destination rates. A typed `ShippingError` class carries structured error codes. Tests live in `.tmp/calculateShipping.test.ts`.

### Key decisions
- **`ShippingError` class** — extends `Error` with a `readonly code: "INVALID_WEIGHT" | "UNKNOWN_DESTINATION"` field. No externa
